verdicts.reasoning: The value from get_option('wsp_add_nofollow') is sanitized by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1() which strips everything except '0' and '1' via preg_replace('/[^01]/', '', $str). The sanitized value is then used only in a ternary comparison ($wsp_add_nofollow==1), and the echo outputs either the literal string ' checked="checked"' or an empty string. The tainted variable itself is never injected into HTML output. No XSS is possible.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_is_display_post_multiple_time') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Additionally, the settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_cpt_page') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_cpt_post') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_cpt_archive') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_cpt_author') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_cpt_'.$cpt->name) is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison (=='1'), never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_exclude_taxonomy_'.$taxonomy_name) is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison (=='1'), never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_is_exclude_password_protected') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

Same pattern as xss-001. Value from get_option('wsp_is_display_copyright') is sanitized to only '0'/'1' by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1(). Used only in ternary comparison, never directly echoed. Settings page requires 'administrator' capability.

The value from get_option('WPLANG') is used only inside in_array() to produce a boolean ($is_fr). The $url_author variable is then set to one of two hardcoded literal strings ('http://tonyarchambeau.com/' or 'http://en.tonyarchambeau.com/') via a ternary on $is_fr. The tainted WPLANG value never reaches the echo statement -- only hardcoded URLs do. Additionally, the output is passed through wsp_esc_some_html_tags() which applies wp_kses(). The settings page also requires 'administrator' capability. No XSS possible.

summary: All 11 flagged taint flows are false positives. The plugin (WP Sitemap Page v1.9.5) has a small attack surface: no AJAX handlers, no REST API routes, no file uploads, no unserialize calls, no eval/exec. The single SQL query (line 539) uses no user input. All settings are protected by the 'administrator' capability check via add_options_page(). The flagged XSS flows all follow the same pattern: values from get_option() are sanitized by wsp_esc_to_keep_0_or_1() (which strips everything except '0' and '1'), then used only in ternary comparisons where the echoed output is a hardcoded literal string -- the tainted value is never injected into HTML. Flow xss-011 uses get_option('WPLANG') only in an in_array() check that produces a boolean, which then selects between two hardcoded URLs.
feedback: The taint analyzer lacks understanding of ternary comparison semantics. When a tainted variable is used only as the condition in a ternary expression (e.g., $var==1 ? 'literal' : ''), the output is a literal string, not the tainted value. The analyzer should recognize that comparison operators break taint propagation when neither branch of the ternary contains the tainted variable. Similarly, in_array() with a tainted needle and hardcoded haystack produces a boolean that carries no taint. All 11 findings suffered from this same class of false positive.
